title: Human-in-the-Loop
subtitle: Human-Agent Interaction
description: How to pause agent execution at critical checkpoints, clarify with users, and obtain approvals. A key aspect of human-agent collaboration.
sub_problems: Pause point design: which operations require human confirmation
Clarification requests: how agents ask users when uncertain
Approval workflows: approval mechanisms for risky operations
Feedback integration: how user feedback influences subsequent execution
Multi-turn interaction: multi-turn human-agent dialogue in complex tasks
best_practices: CLARIFY→PLAN→ACT priority: agents must clarify first when uncertain, strictly prohibit acting then asking
Structured clarification types: use enumerated types (missing_info, etc.) rather than free text for easier frontend UI rendering
Middleware interception over node-level hardcoding: wrap_tool_call hooks decouple clarification logic from business logic
interrupt() over threading.Event: non-blocking, supports persistence, scalable to multiple interruption points
Automatic timeout handling: default behavior when users don't respond for extended periods, prevents permanent task suspension

values.DeerFlow-Interrupt: SqliteSaver persistence, supports process restart recovery
single layer: interrupt() within graph node

solutions.title: ClarificationMiddleware with five-type proactive clarification
solutions.description: Three-layer architecture for proactive clarification: ask_clarification @tool at tool layer (5 types: missing_info/ambiguous_requirement/approach_choice/risk_confirmation/suggestion) → ClarificationMiddleware at middleware layer (wrap_tool_call interception, _format_clarification_message formats icon-labeled messages, Command(goto=END) interrupts graph execution) → Prompt layer (<clarification_system> block in lead_agent/prompt.py enforces CLARIFY→PLAN→ACT workflow priority). Frontend detects and renders clarification UI via isClarificationToolMessage().

solutions.title: LangGraph interrupt() with Command(resume=...) native interruption recovery
solutions.description: Replace threading.Event blocking with native LangGraph interrupt(): _planner_node calls interrupt({type: confirm_outline}) to automatically interrupt graph execution and persist checkpoint, frontend displays interrupt data, user action triggers POST /api/tasks/resume, backend resumes graph execution via Command(resume=action). Supports SqliteSaver persistence for task recovery after process restart.
